content.full_text:
/i

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 27, 1993

Doug Vagim
P.O. Box 4042
Fresno, CA 93744

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-281

Dear Mr. Vagim:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act ("the Act").1/

QUESTIONS

1. Following a special election, may your Assembly special
election committee continue to receive contributions?

2. May you receive contributions into your Supervisor
committee and then transfer those contributions to your Assembly
special election committee to pay its outstanding debts?

3. May your Supervisor committee receive contributions from
persons that made the maximum permissible contributions to your
special election committee?

CONCLUSIONS

1. Following a special election, your Assembly special
election committee may continue to receive contributions as long
as such contributions are received from persons that have not made
the maximum permissible contributions in the primary and general
elections.

2. Contributions may be transferred from your Supervisor
committee to your Assembly special election committee as long as
such contributions would not exceed the contribution limits
imposed during the special primary and general elections.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

A-93-281
Page 2


     3.  Your Supervisor committee may receive contributions from
persons that made the maximum contributions permissible to your
special election committee.  However, these contributions may not
be transferred to your Assembly special election committee.

                              FACTS

     You are a county supervisor and an unsuccessful candidate in
a special election for the California State Assembly.  You have
two committees, one designated for the special election and one
designated for your county supervisor position.  During the
special election, you transferred contributions from your
Supervisor committee to your special election committee and some
individuals made the maximum contributions permissible under the
special election contribution limits.  The Assembly special
election primary was held in March 1993 and the general election
was held in April 1993.

                            ANALYSIS

     In the case of  Service Employees International Union, AFL-
CIO, CLC, et al. v.FPPC (9th Cir. 1992) 955 F.2d 1312, cert.den.
112 S.Ct. 3056 and 112 S.Ct. 3057, the court invalidated the
contribution limitations in the Act based on fiscal years.  The
court did not invalidate the special election and special runoff
election contribution limitations.  The court also allowed
candidates to transfer campaign funds among their own campaign
committees.  Therefore, a candidate with more than one campaign
committee may transfer contributions from a regular election
committee to his special election committee as long as the
transfer does not cause the total amount contributed by any person
to the special election committee to exceed the contribution
limitations of Section 85305.

     The Commission has adopted Regulation 18535 (copy enclosed)
to clarify that following a special election, a special election
committee may continue to receive contributions as long as such
contributions do not exceed the contribution limits.  Therefore,
persons who did not contribute the maximum permissible during the
primary and general election cycles may now make contributions to
the Assembly committee as long as those contributions, when
accumulated with contributions previously made or transferred to the
Assembly committee, do not exceed the limits imposed by Section
85305. (Bell Advice Letter, No. A-93-234, copy enclosed.)  The
regulation also specifies the reporting obligations for any
transferred contributions.

     A candidate's controlled committee for a regular election can
receive contributions from persons that previously made the
maximum permissible contributions to the candidate's special
election committee.  However, such contributions may not be
transferred to the special election committee.

A-93-281
Page 3




     If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Wayne Ordos
                                        Executive Director
                                        [signature]
                                        by: Lynda Cassady
                                            Assistant Division Chief
                                            Technical Assistance Division
